preview_text:
The model favors Mexico at 71 percent, with Czech Republic given just 7 percent chance of victory. This is a heavy lean toward the home-nation side, whose win probability towers over the underdog's modest prospects.

The spread reflects a fairly decisive model expectation: Mexico is heavily favored, though a draw at 23 percent remains a meaningful outcome. The gap between a Mexican win and a Czech Republic win is substantial, suggesting the model sees this as a low-competitive matchup rather than an even contest. A draw is plausible enough to merit inclusion in any scenario planning, but the modal outcome tilts sharply in one direction.

The model and sportsbooks diverge materially here. Bookmakers strip the Mexico win down to 52 percent—19 percentage points below the model—while pricing Czech Republic at 21 percent, nearly triple the model's estimate. Polymarket prices fall between the two, closer to the sportsbook consensus. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better reflects true match probability, but the divergence is large enough to warrant attention from anyone using these forecasts.
